Manufacturing Guide

Cut and Sew Manufacturer: From Your Design to Finished Garment

✎ FABRIOZA Team 📅 July 1, 2026 ☕ 10 min read
Home / Blog / Cut and Sew Manufacturer: From Your Design to Finished Garment

When your design is unique — a specific silhouette, unconventional construction, or proprietary pattern — cut and sew manufacturing is the only way to achieve it. At FABRIOZA, we offer full cut-and-sew production from pattern making to finished garment, with MOQ starting at 50 pieces.

What Is Cut and Sew Manufacturing?

Cut and sew means garments are built from raw fabric rather than decorating pre-made blanks. The process: your tech pack → pattern creation → fabric sourcing → marker making → fabric cutting → sewing → finishing → quality control. Every step is custom to your specifications.

When to Use Cut and Sew vs Other Models

Choose Cut & SewUse Pre-Made Blanks
Unique silhouettes or fitsStandard t-shirts, hoodies
Custom fabric requirementsBasic cotton/poly blends
Complex constructionSimple decoration only
Proprietary designsGeneric designs
Premium brand positioningBudget/convenience focus

What You Need to Brief a Cut and Sew Manufacturer

A tech pack is ideal — it includes technical sketches, measurement specifications, fabric details, construction notes, and trim requirements. If you don't have a tech pack, send us a reference garment or detailed sketches. Our design team creates production specifications from your references.

Do You Need a Tech Pack?

For fully custom garments: yes, a tech pack ensures accuracy. For standard styles with modifications: we can work from design references. Download our free tech pack template to get started.

FABRIOZA's Cut and Sew Process

  1. Tech Pack Review: We analyze feasibility and recommend optimizations
  2. Pattern Making: CAD patterns created and graded across all sizes
  3. Fabric Sourcing: 200+ certified fabrics available
  4. Sample Development: First sample in typically 1-3 weeks
  5. Bulk Production: 15 production lines, 2,500 units/day capacity

Fabrics Available

200+ certified fabrics including cotton, polyester, spandex, bamboo, organic, recycled, and technical performance fabrics. Full traceability on every roll.

MOQ and Pricing

50 pieces per style. Prices range from $8-25 per unit depending on complexity, fabric, and construction.

Bring Your Garment Design to Life

Cut and sew from 50 pieces. Any design. Any fabric. Free quote in 24 hours.

Send Your Design Brief →

Frequently Asked Questions

What is a tech pack and do I need one?

A tech pack is a technical document with measurements, materials, and construction details. We can work with sketches, reference garments, or descriptions if you don't have one.

How long does cut and sew sampling take?

First sample: typically 1-3 weeks. Revisions: typically 3-7 days each. Most clients approve within 2-3 samples.

What fabrics can you source?

200+ certified fabrics including cotton, polyester, spandex, bamboo, organic, recycled, and technical performance fabrics.

Is cut and sew more expensive than blanks?

Yes, 15-30% more per unit, but the quality, fit, and brand differentiation are significantly higher.

Can you copy a garment I send?

Yes, send us a reference garment and we'll create a perfect pattern and sample.
